column2: laus ejus in ecclēsiā sanctōrum. ^ Sing ye to the Lord a new canticle~
column3: let his praise be in the church of the saints.

column1: 2 ^ Laetētur Isrāel in eō quī fēcit eum, et fīliī Siōnis exsultent in rēge suō. ^ Let Israel rejoice in him that made him, and let the children of Sion be joyful in their king.

column2: in timpanō et psaltēriō psallant eī. ^ Let them praise his name in choir~
column3: let them sing to him with the timbrel and the psaltery.

column1: 4 ^ Quia benēplacitum est Dominō in populō suō, et exaltābit mansuētōs in salūtem. ^ For the Lord is well pleased with his people, and he will exalt the meek unto salvation.

column2: laetābuntur in cubīlibus suīs. ^ The saints shall rejoice in glory~
column3: they shall be joyful in their beds.

column1: 6 ^ Exaltātiōnēs Deī in gutture eōrum, et gladiī ancipitēs in manibus eōrum~
column2: ^ The high praises of God shall be in their mouths, and two-edged swords in their hands~

column1: 7 ^ ad faciendam vindictam in nātiōnibus, increpātiōnēs in populīs~
column2: ^ to execute vengeance upon the nations, chastisements among the people~

column1: 8 ^ ad alligandōs rēgēs eōrum in compedibus, et nōbilēs eōrum in manicīs ferreīs~
column2: ^ to bind their kings with fetters, and their nobles with manacles of iron~

column1: 9 ^ ut faciant in eīs jūdicium conscriptum~
column2: glōria haec est omnibus sanctīs ejus. Allēlūja. ^ to execute upon them the judgment that is written~
column3: this glory is to all his saints. Alleluia.
